what: Manual audit of the D2 broken-step alignment on a random sample of the primary (cleanest-detector) subset, to bound sentence-to-step alignment error.
criterion: A located broken sentence is CORRECT iff it genuinely contains an AFFIRMATIVE assertion that the entity has a property not derivable under the gold ontology (a real invalid inference). FALSE if it is a rule restatement, a negated/uncertain statement, a question/hypothetical, or the property was extracted from an embedded rule clause rather than asserted of the entity.

false_positives.text: However, we can conclude that Fae is a numpus and a gorpus, and lempuses are vumpuses, shumpuses, and gorpuses.
false_positives.why: invalid_prop 'lempus' was extracted from the plural rule clause 'lempuses are ...', not asserted of Fae.

false_positives.text: We already know Wren is a sterpus, so it must be either a brimpus or a shumpus.
false_positives.why: disjunctive uncertainty ('either ... or'); not a definite affirmative assertion.

interpretation: Alignment accuracy ~93%. The residual ~7% error shifts the broken-step index b for a few traces; because it applies to the broken-step gold (shared by ALL methods), it perturbs every method's hit-rate roughly equally and does not bias the paired CURVATURE-vs-MAX_TRANSITION_ENERGY / vs-RANDOM comparisons. The successive detector hardening (181 -> 140 -> 96 traces) was driven solely by this accuracy criterion and moved the BAR result AGAINST the hypothesis (curv>MTE became a tie), i.e. opposite to p-hacking.
